Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx

Class FlixelObject

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Disposable, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable, FlixelDebugDrawable, FlixelAngleable, FlixelDestroyable, FlixelDrawable, FlixelKillable, FlixelPositional, FlixelShakeable, FlixelUpdatable, FlixelVisible, IFlixelBasic
Direct Known Subclasses:
FlixelSprite
public class FlixelObject extends FlixelBasic implements FlixelDebugDrawable, FlixelPositional, FlixelShakeable
The base class for all visual/spatial objects in Flixel. Extends FlixelBasic with position (x, y), dimensions (width, height), rotation (angle), and a full kinematic physics model (velocity, acceleration, drag, collision flags).

Most games interact with this through FlixelSprite, which adds graphical capabilities on top of this spatial foundation.

Collision

Use Flixel.overlap() and Flixel.collide() for overlap/separation checks. The static separate(FlixelObject, FlixelObject) method resolves overlaps by adjusting positions and velocities.

    Class
    Description
    static final class 
    FlixelObject.DirectionFlags
    Bit flags for collision and touch sides.

    Fields

    Field
    Description
    static final float
    SEPARATE_BIAS
    Maximum number of pixels two objects can intersect before separate(FlixelObject, FlixelObject) gives up when using overlap checks.

    Constructor
    Description
    FlixelObject()
    Creates a new FlixelObject with the default position (0, 0) and size (0, 0).

    Creates a new FlixelObject with the given position and size (0, 0).
    FlixelObject(float x, float y, float width, float height)
    Creates a new FlixelObject with the given position and size.

    Description

    changeAngle(float deltaDegrees)
    Adds deltaDegrees to the current rotation angle.

    Adds dy to the current Y position.

    computeOverlapX(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2, boolean checkMaxOverlap)
    Computes the overlap between two objects on the X axis and updates their touching flags.
    static float

    Computes the overlap between two objects on the Y axis and updates their touching flags.
    float
    computeVelocity(float velocity, float acceleration, float drag, float max, float elapsed)
    Internal function for computing velocity after acceleration and drag.

    destroy()
    Cleans up this object so it can be garbage-collected.

    getAccelerationX()
    Horizontal acceleration in pixels per second squared.

    Vertical acceleration in pixels per second squared.

    getAngle()
    The angle in degrees of this object.
    float
    getAngularAcceleration()
    Rotational acceleration in degrees per second squared.
    float
    getAngularDrag()
    Rotational drag in degrees per second squared.
    float
    getAngularVelocity()
    Rotational speed in degrees per second.
    float[]
    getDebugBoundingBoxColor()
    Returns the appropriate debug color based on collision state: Override color if set Blue when allowCollisions == NONE Green when immovable or partial collision Red (normal/solid) otherwise
    float
    getDebugDrawX(FlixelCamera cam)
    X position of the debug box in the same world space as FlixelCamera projection during draw (includes scroll-factor / parallax).
    float
    getDebugDrawY(FlixelCamera cam)
    Y position of the debug box in the same world space as FlixelCamera projection during draw.
    float
    getDebugHeight()
    Height of the bounding box in world pixels.
    float
    getDebugWidth()
    Width of the bounding box in world pixels.
    float
    getDebugX()
    X position of the bounding box in world space.
    float
    getDebugY()
    Y position of the bounding box in world space.
    float
    getDragX()
    Deceleration applied when FlixelPositional.getAccelerationX() is zero.
    float
    getDragY()
    Deceleration applied when FlixelPositional.getAccelerationY() is zero.
    float
    getElasticity()
     
    float
    getHeight()
    Height of this object's hitbox.
    float
    getLastX()
    X position at the start of the current frame, before motion.
    float
    getLastY()
    Y position at the start of the current frame, before motion.

    getMass()

    getMaxVelocityY()

    getMidpointX()
    Returns the center X coordinate of this object.
    float
    getMidpointY()
    Returns the center Y coordinate of this object.
    boolean
    getMoves()
    When true, updateMotion(float) runs each frame on FlixelObject.
    float
    getScrollX()
    Parallax scroll factor on X (1 means the object moves with the camera like a normal world object).
    float
    getScrollY()
    Parallax scroll factor on Y (1 means the object moves with the camera like a normal world object).
    float
    getShakeHeight()
    Height used to scale FlixelShakeUnit.FRACTION on the Y axis.
    float
    getShakeWidth()
    Width used to scale FlixelShakeUnit.FRACTION on the X axis.
    float
    getShakeX()
    Current shake channel X (for example graphic offset or world X).
    float
    getShakeY()
    Current shake channel Y.

    X position of the upper left corner of this object in world space.

    getY()

    inWorldBounds()
    Returns true if the object is within the world bounds configured on the global manager class.
    boolean
    isImmovable()
    When true, this object will not be moved by collision resolution.
    boolean
    isSolid()
    Convenience accessor, returns true when allowCollisions is not FlixelObject.DirectionFlags.NONE.
    boolean
    isTouching(int direction)
    Returns true if this object is currently touching the given surface(s).
    boolean
    justTouched(int direction)
    Returns true if this object just started touching the given surface(s) this frame (was not touching last frame).
    boolean
    overlaps(FlixelObject other)
    Checks if this object's AABB overlaps another FlixelObject.
    boolean
    overlapsPoint(float px, float py)
    Checks if a point in world space overlaps this object's AABB.
    void
    reset(float x, float y)
    Resets this object to a new position, reviving it and clearing physics state.

    separate(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2)
    Separates two overlapping objects by adjusting positions and velocities.

    Separates two overlapping objects on the X axis.

    Internal function for updating the position and speed of this object using a velocity-verlet style integration.

    • SEPARATE_BIAS

      public static final float SEPARATE_BIAS
      Maximum number of pixels two objects can intersect before separate(FlixelObject, FlixelObject) gives up when using overlap checks.

      Creates a new FlixelObject with the default position (0, 0) and size (0, 0).

      height - The height.

  • Method Details

    • update

      public void update(float elapsed)
      Updates this object's collision bookkeeping and, when moves is true, calls updateMotion(float).
      Specified by:
      update in interface FlixelUpdatable
      Overrides:
      update in class FlixelBasic
      Parameters:
      elapsed - Seconds since the last frame.

    • destroy

      public void destroy()
      Description copied from class: FlixelBasic
      Cleans up this object so it can be garbage-collected. A destroyed FlixelBasic should not be used anymore. Use FlixelBasic.kill() if you only want to disable it temporarily and FlixelBasic.revive() it later.

      Override this function to clean up any resources used by this object, such as textures, fonts, sounds, etc.

      This function is called automatically when FlixelBasic.dispose() or FlixelBasic.reset() is executed, so you don't need to call it manually.

      Specified by:
      destroy in interface FlixelDestroyable
      Overrides:
      destroy in class FlixelBasic
      See Also:
      • FlixelBasic.dispose()
      • FlixelBasic.reset()

      public void setMoves(boolean moves)
      Enables or disables automatic motion integration on FlixelObject.

      setMoves in interface FlixelPositional

      public boolean isSolid()
      Convenience accessor, returns true when allowCollisions is not FlixelObject.DirectionFlags.NONE.

      public void setSolid(boolean solid)

    • isImmovable

      public boolean isImmovable()
      When true, this object will not be moved by collision resolution. Other objects will still be pushed away from it.
      Specified by:
      isImmovable in interface FlixelPositional
      Returns:
      Current immovable flag.

    • setImmovable

      public void setImmovable(boolean immovable)
      Sets whether collision resolution may move this object.
      Specified by:
      setImmovable in interface FlixelPositional
      Parameters:
      immovable - true to freeze this object during separation.

    • getShakeX

      public float getShakeX()
      Current shake channel X (for example graphic offset or world X).
      Specified by:
      getShakeX in interface FlixelShakeable
      Returns:
      Shake X before jitter is applied.

      public float getShakeY()
      Current shake channel Y.
      Specified by:
      getShakeY in interface FlixelShakeable
      Returns:
      Shake Y before jitter is applied.

    • setShake

      public void setShake(float x, float y)
      Sets the shake channel to absolute coordinates (typically base plus jitter).
      Specified by:
      setShake in interface FlixelShakeable
      Parameters:
      x - New shake X.
      y - New shake Y.

    • getShakeWidth

      public float getShakeWidth()
      Width used to scale FlixelShakeUnit.FRACTION on the X axis. Values 0 or less mean the tween treats intensity like plain pixels on that axis.
      Specified by:
      getShakeWidth in interface FlixelShakeable
      Returns:
      Reference width for fractional shake, or non-positive to fall back.

    • getShakeHeight

      public float getShakeHeight()
      Height used to scale FlixelShakeUnit.FRACTION on the Y axis. Values 0 or less mean the tween treats intensity like plain pixels on that axis.
      Specified by:
      getShakeHeight in interface FlixelShakeable
      Returns:
      Reference height for fractional shake, or non-positive to fall back.

    • getElasticity

      public float getElasticity()

    • setElasticity

      public void setElasticity(float elasticity)

    • getMass

      public float getMass()

    • setMass

      public void setMass(float mass)

    • isTouching

      public boolean isTouching(int direction)
      Returns true if this object is currently touching the given surface(s). Flags are set by Flixel.collide(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic, java.util.function.BiConsumer<me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject>) and reset each frame.
      Parameters:
      direction - One or more direction flags ORed together.

    • justTouched

      public boolean justTouched(int direction)
      Returns true if this object just started touching the given surface(s) this frame (was not touching last frame).
      Parameters:
      direction - One or more direction flags ORed together.

    • reset

      public void reset(float x, float y)
      Resets this object to a new position, reviving it and clearing physics state.
      Parameters:
      x - New X position.
      y - New Y position.

    • overlaps

      public boolean overlaps(FlixelObject other)
      Checks if this object's AABB overlaps another FlixelObject.
      Parameters:
      other - The other object.
      Returns:
      true if the two AABBs overlap.

    • overlapsPoint

      public boolean overlapsPoint(float px, float py)
      Checks if a point in world space overlaps this object's AABB.
      Parameters:
      px - X coordinate.
      py - Y coordinate.
      Returns:
      true if the point is inside.

    • inWorldBounds

      public boolean inWorldBounds()
      Returns true if the object is within the world bounds configured on the global manager class.

    • updateMotion

      public void updateMotion(float elapsed)
      Internal function for updating the position and speed of this object using a velocity-verlet style integration.
      Parameters:
      elapsed - Seconds elapsed since the last frame.

    • computeVelocity

      public float computeVelocity(float velocity, float acceleration, float drag, float max, float elapsed)
      Internal function for computing velocity after acceleration and drag.
      Parameters:
      velocity - Current velocity.
      acceleration - Acceleration to apply.
      drag - Drag to apply when acceleration is zero.
      max - Maximum allowed velocity.
      elapsed - Seconds elapsed since the last frame.
      Returns:
      Updated velocity.

    • separate

      public static boolean separate(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2)
      Separates two overlapping objects by adjusting positions and velocities. Calls separateX(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject) then separateY(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject).
      Parameters:
      object1 - First object.
      object2 - Second object.
      Returns:
      true if the objects were overlapping and were separated.

    • separateX

      public static boolean separateX(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2)
      Separates two overlapping objects on the X axis.
      Returns:
      true if the objects overlapped and were separated on X.

    • separateY

      public static boolean separateY(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2)
      Separates two overlapping objects on the Y axis.
      Returns:
      true if the objects overlapped and were separated on Y.

    • computeOverlapX

      public static float computeOverlapX(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2, boolean checkMaxOverlap)
      Computes the overlap between two objects on the X axis and updates their touching flags. Returns 0 if they do not overlap or if their allowCollisions flags disallow collision on the overlapping sides.
      Parameters:
      object1 - First object.
      object2 - Second object.
      checkMaxOverlap - Whether to reject overlaps greater than combined movement + SEPARATE_BIAS.
      Returns:
      Signed overlap in pixels (positive means object1's right side penetrates object2's left side).

    • computeOverlapY

      public static float computeOverlapY(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2, boolean checkMaxOverlap)
      Computes the overlap between two objects on the Y axis and updates their touching flags. Same semantics as computeOverlapX(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, boolean).
      Parameters:
      object1 - First object.
      object2 - Second object.
      checkMaxOverlap - Whether to reject overlaps greater than combined movement + SEPARATE_BIAS.
      Returns:
      Signed overlap in pixels (positive means object1's bottom side penetrates object2's top side).

    • updateTouchingFlags

      public static boolean updateTouchingFlags(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2)
      Checks if two objects overlap and updates their touching flags without separating them.
      Parameters:
      object1 - First object.
      object2 - Second object.
      Returns:
      true if the two objects are touching.

    • getDebugX

      public float getDebugX()
      Description copied from interface: FlixelDebugDrawable
      X position of the bounding box in world space.
      Specified by:
      getDebugX in interface FlixelDebugDrawable

    • getDebugY

      public float getDebugY()
      Description copied from interface: FlixelDebugDrawable
      Y position of the bounding box in world space.
      Specified by:
      getDebugY in interface FlixelDebugDrawable

    • getDebugDrawX

      public float getDebugDrawX(FlixelCamera cam)
      Description copied from interface: FlixelDebugDrawable
      X position of the debug box in the same world space as FlixelCamera projection during draw (includes scroll-factor / parallax). Defaults to FlixelDebugDrawable.getDebugX().
      Specified by:
      getDebugDrawX in interface FlixelDebugDrawable
      Parameters:
      cam - The game camera used for this debug pass.

    • getDebugDrawY

      public float getDebugDrawY(FlixelCamera cam)
      Description copied from interface: FlixelDebugDrawable
      Y position of the debug box in the same world space as FlixelCamera projection during draw. Defaults to FlixelDebugDrawable.getDebugY().
      Specified by:
      getDebugDrawY in interface FlixelDebugDrawable
      Parameters:
      cam - The game camera used for this debug pass.

    • getDebugWidth

      public float getDebugWidth()
      Description copied from interface: FlixelDebugDrawable
      Width of the bounding box in world pixels.
      Specified by:
      getDebugWidth in interface FlixelDebugDrawable

    • getDebugHeight

      public float getDebugHeight()
      Description copied from interface: FlixelDebugDrawable
      Height of the bounding box in world pixels.
      Specified by:
      getDebugHeight in interface FlixelDebugDrawable

    • getDebugBoundingBoxColor

      public float[] getDebugBoundingBoxColor()
      Returns the appropriate debug color based on collision state:
      • Override color if set
      • Blue when allowCollisions == NONE
      • Green when immovable or partial collision
      • Red (normal/solid) otherwise
      Specified by:
      getDebugBoundingBoxColor in interface FlixelDebugDrawable

    • setDebugBoundingBoxColor

      public void setDebugBoundingBoxColor(float[] rgba)
      Forces a specific debug bounding box color, overriding the automatic selection. Pass null to re-enable automatic color selection.
      Parameters:
      rgba - 4-element float array [r, g, b, a], or null.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class FlixelBasic
